zh.Hulme Walfield, Cheshire, England: 英國爾赫爾姆 瓦爾費爾德
zh.Great Budworth, Cheshire, England: 英國爾格亞特 布德沃斯
zh.lifeSummary: 當Robert le Grosvenor於1310年之前出生在英國時，他的父親Robert le Grosvenor34歲，母親Margery33歲。他於1327年與Emma de Mobberley在英國爾結婚。他們至少是2個兒子和2個女兒的父母。他去世在英國爾格亞特 布德沃斯，埋葬在英國爾格亞特 布德沃斯。

zh-hans.Hulme Walfield, Cheshire, England: 英国爾赫爾姆 瓦爾費爾德
zh-hans.Great Budworth, Cheshire, England: 英国爾格亞特 布德沃斯
zh-hans.lifeSummary: 当Robert le Grosvenor于1310年之前出生在英国时，他的父亲Robert le Grosvenor34岁，母亲Margery33岁。他于1327年与Emma de Mobberley在英国爾结婚。他们至少是2个儿子和2个女儿的父母。他去世在英国爾格亞特 布德沃斯，埋葬在英国爾格亞特 布德沃斯。

nameMeanings.name: le
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Vietnamese Thanh, Hung, Minh, Dung, Tuan, Hai, Hoa, Thuy, Son, Anh, Long, Hoang.  Chinese Hong, Sang, Chi, Dong, Tong, Lai, Hang, Man, Han, Ho, Phoung, Chan.  Korean Tam, Phong, Nam, Thai, Tuong, Chung, Thach, Tinh, Manh, Tham, Uyen, Vang.

Chinese: Mandarin form of the surname 樂, meaning ‘happiness’ in Chinese: from the first element of the personal name Le Fu (樂父), style name of Yan, son of the Duke Dai of Song (ruler of the state of Song, reigned 799–766  BC ). Yan's grandson Le Ju (樂莒) inherited Le (樂) as his surname.

Chinese: Mandarin form of the surname 勒: (i) from the second element of the placename Shu-Le (疏勒), an ancient state (located in present-day Xinjiang Uyghur autonomous region) during the Western Han dynasty (206  BC –25  AD ). (ii) a surname near the Bohai Sea region during the Han dynasty (206  BC –220  AD ). (iii) a surname traced back to the Hun ethnic group in northern China during the Jin dynasty (265–420  AD ).

variants.name: Yue
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Chinese: Chi, Feng, Gang, Wei, Chun, Guo, Jin, Kin, Ping, Yan, Yun, Zhang, Chau, Du, Hau, Hung, My, Sau.

Mandarin form of the surname 岳 (or 嶽), meaning ‘high mountain’ in Chinese: traced back to Si Yue (四岳 or 四嶽), said to be the title of an official post in charge of sacrificial rituals on the site of famous mountains during the reign of the legendary Emperor Yao (c. 24th century BC ) and Emperor Shun (c. 23rd century BC ).

variants.name: Lok
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Chinese Ping, Tat, Wing, Ying Lin, Chi Ming, Ching Wah, Chun, Chung Man, Han, Kam, Kam Tim, Kin.
Chinese: Cantonese form of the surname 樂, see   Le   1.
Chinese: Cantonese form of the surnames 陸, 鹿, 逯 and 禄, see   Lu   5 and (7-9).

variants.name: Li
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Chinese Wei, Ming, Li, Jian, Hong, Yan, Ping, Jing, Yong, Ying, Hui, Jin, Min, Chung.

Chinese: Mandarin form of the surname 李, meaning ‘plum’: (i) said to have been adopted in place of the Chinese surname 理 (also pronounced Li; meaning ‘rules’ in Chinese) by Li Zhen (利貞), son of an official called Li Zheng (理徵) in the late Shang dynasty (1600–1046  BC ). It is said that Li Zhen's father incurred the displeasure of King Zhou of Shang (died 1046  BC ) and Li Zhen fled to a wasteland area, where he survived on wild plums. After his return to civilization, he changed his surname to Li (李 ‘plum’), a word with the same pronunciation. The original surname, 理, is said to be from Li Guan (理官), the name of a post of officials in charge of legislation in ancient China. Li Er (李耳), also known as Lao Tzu or Lao Zi, traditionally regarded as the founder of Taoism, is believed to have been a descendant of Li Zhen. (ii) adopted as a surname by the Chi Li (叱李) family from the Xianbei ethnic group during the Northern Wei dynasty (386–534  AD ). (iii) in the early years of the Tang dynasty (618–907  AD ), sixteen high officials with different surnames were granted the surname Li (李), the surname of the royal family of the Tang dynasty, by Emperor Taizong of Tang (died 649  BC ), because of their contributions to the establishment of the Tang regime. (iv) the surname can also be traced back to several minority ethnic groups in ancient China.

Chinese: Mandarin form of the surname 黎, meaning ‘black’ or ‘multitude’ in ancient Chinese: (i) from Li (黎), the name of an ancient state (located in Changzhi in Shanxi province) during the Shang dynasty (1600–1046  BC ), which was annexed in the Western Zhou dynasty (1046–771  BC ) and later granted to Fengshu, also known as Li Hou (黎侯, 'Marquis Li'). (ii) adopted as a surname by the Su Li (素黎) family, members of the Xianbei ethnic group, during the Northern Wei dynasty (386–534  AD ).

nameMeanings.name: Grosvenor
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: English (of Norman origin): from French gros ‘big, great’ + veneur ‘hunter’.

History: This is the name of one of the wealthiest families in Britain, which holds the title Duke of Westminster. They have been long established in Cheshire, with strong links with the city of Chester. One of the earliest recorded bearers of the name was Robert le Grosvenor of Budworth, who was granted lands by the Earl of Chester in 1160. The family's fortunes were founded by Thomas Grosvenor (born 1656), who in 1677 married an heiress, Mary Davies, whose inheritance included Ebury Farm, Middlesex. This now forms an area of central London that includes Grosvenor Square and Belgrave Square.

variants.name: Gravenor
meanings: English (of Norman origin): nickname from Old French grand ‘big, tall’ + veneor ‘hunter’. Compare Grosvenor .

nameMeanings.name: Robert
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: One of the many French names of Germanic origin that were introduced into Britain by the Normans; it has since remained in continuous use. It is derived from the nearly synonymous elements hrōd ‘fame’ + berht ‘bright, famous’, and had a native Old English predecessor of similar form (Hreodbeorht), which was supplanted by the Norman name. Two dukes of Normandy in the 11th century bore the name: the father of William the Conqueror (sometimes identified with the legendary Robert the Devil), and his eldest son. It was borne also by three kings of Scotland, notably Robert the Bruce ( 1274–1329 ), who freed Scotland from English domination. The altered short form Bob is very common, but Hob and Dob, which were common in the Middle Ages and gave rise to surnames, are extinct. See also Rupert .

historicEvents.title: Famine Strikes Europe
historicEvents.description: A famine caused by cold winters and wet summers across Europe caused millions to die due to starvation. Desperation from hunger caused high levels of crime, stampedes in bread lines in London, infanticide, and even cannibalism throughout Europe.

historicEvents.title: First War of Scottish Independence
historicEvents.description: The First War of Scottish Independence began after England invaded Scotland in 1296. The Treaty of Edinburgh was signed in 1328 which declared peace between the two kingdoms and brought the war to an end.

historicEvents.title: Wycliffe's Bible
historicEvents.description: John Wycliffe, English theologian and professor at the University of Oxford, completed a translation of the Bible from the Latin Vulgate into Middle English. This translation is known as Wycliffe's Bible and was completed around 1382.

en.lifeSummary: When Robert le Grosvenor was born before 1310, in England, his father, Robert le Grosvenor, was 34 and his mother, Margery, was 33. He married Emma de Mobberley in 1327, in Cheshire, England. They were the parents of at least 2 sons and 2 daughters. He died in Great Budworth, Cheshire, England, and was buried in Great Budworth, Cheshire, England.

fr.lifeSummary: Lorsque Robert le Grosvenor est né avant 1310, à Angleterre, son père, Robert le Grosvenor, avait 34 ans et sa mère, Margery, avait 33 ans. Il a épousé Emma de Mobberley en 1327, à Cheshire, Angleterre. Ils ont eu au moins 2 fils et 2 filles. Il est décédé à Great Budworth, Cheshire, Angleterre, et a été enterré à Great Budworth, Cheshire, Angleterre.

de.lifeSummary: Als Robert le Grosvenor vor 1310 in England geboren wurde, war sein Vater Robert le Grosvenor 34 und seine Mutter Margery 33. Er hat 1327 in Cheshire, England Emma de Mobberley geheiratet. Sie hatten mindestens 2 Söhne und 2 Töchter. Er starb in Great Budworth, Cheshire, England und wurde in Great Budworth, Cheshire, England begraben.

it.lifeSummary: Quando Robert le Grosvenor è nato il prima del 1310 nel England, il suo padre, Robert le Grosvenor, aveva 34 anni e il suo madre, Margery, aveva 33 anni. Lui sposato Emma de Mobberley nel 1327, nel Cheshire, England. Erano genitori di almeno 2 figli e 2 figlie. Morì nel Great Budworth, Cheshire, England, e fu sepolto a Great Budworth, Cheshire, England.

ja.Hulme Walfield, Cheshire, England: イギリスチェシュアイアハルメ ワルフィールド
ja.Cheshire, England: イギリスチェシュアイア
ja.Hulme, Lancashire, England: イギリスランカシュアイアハルメ
ja.Great Budworth, Cheshire, England: イギリスチェシュアイアグレアト ブドウオルス
ja.Holme, Cheshire, England: イギリスイギリスチェシュアイア
ja.lifeSummary: Robert le Grosvenorが1310年以前にイギリスで生まれた時，父Robert le Grosvenorは34歳，母Margeryは33歳であった。1327年にイギリスチェシュアイアでEmma de Mobberleyと結婚した。彼らは少なくとも2人の息子と2人の娘の親であった。彼はイギリスチェシュアイアグレアト ブドウオルスで死去し，イギリスチェシュアイアグレアト ブドウオルスにて埋葬された。

ko.lifeSummary: Robert le Grosvenor(이)가 1310년 이전 영국에서 태어났을 때, 그의 아버지 Robert le Grosvenor(은)는 34세였고, 그의 어머니 Margery(은)는 33세였다. 그는 1327년 영국 체실르에서 Emma de Mobberley(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 두 명의 아들과 두 명의 딸을 두었다. 그는 영국 체실르 그리트 버드워스에서 사망했으며 영국 체실르 그리트 버드워스에 묻혔다.

pt.lifeSummary: Quando Robert le Grosvenor nasceu antes 1310, em Inglaterra, seu pai, Robert le Grosvenor, tinha 34 anos e sua mãe, Margery, tinha 33 anos. Ele casou-se com Emma de Mobberley em 1327, em Cheshire, Inglaterra. Eles tiveram pelo menos 2 filhos e 2 filhas. Ele faleceu em Great Budworth, Cheshire, Inglaterra, e foi sepultado em Great Budworth, Cheshire, Inglaterra.

ru.lifeSummary: Когда Robert le Grosvenor родился перед 1310 г. в местности Англия, его отцу по имени Robert le Grosvenor было 34 года, а матери, которую звали Margery, 33. Он женился на женщине по имени Emma de Mobberley в 1327 г. в местности чешир, Англия. У них было по крайней мере 2 сына и 2 дочери. Он умер в местности грит будворт, чешир, Англия и был похоронен в местности грит будворт, чешир, Англия.

es.lifeSummary: Cuando Robert le Grosvenor nació antes de 1310, en Inglaterra, su papá, Robert le Grosvenor, tenía 34 años y su mamá, Margery, tenía 33 años. Se casó con Emma de Mobberley en 1327, en Cheshire, Inglaterra. Fueron padres de por lo menos 2 hijos y 2 hijas. Murió en Great Budworth, Cheshire, Inglaterra, y fue sepultado en Great Budworth, Cheshire, Inglaterra.
